text: Managing a team effectively requires more than just giving orders. Good communication is essential, as it ensures everyone understands their tasks and goals. Delegation is also crucial; assigning tasks based on each member's strengths can improve productivity and build trust. Regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued, while motivation keeps them engaged, even during challenging projects. When conflicts arise, it's important to address them quickly and fairly, listening to all sides to find a solution. By focusing on these key skills, a manager can create a positive and efficient work environment.
